rules.notes: Visa on arrival for Indian nationals holding a valid US visa, UK or Schengen residence; otherwise apply for a pre-arranged e-visa.
Electronic Travel Authorization (ETA) must be obtained online before arrival.

Indian nationals need a B1/B2 visitor visa. Apply online (DS-160), pay the fee, and attend an in-person interview at a U.S. consulate. Visa is typically valid for 10 years; stay is determined by CBP on entry.

Indian passport holders require a UK Standard Visitor visa applied for in advance online with biometrics submitted at a visa application centre. Allows tourism, business and family visits for up to 6 months per entry.

Indian passport holders require a Schengen short-stay (Type C) visa to enter France. Apply via VFS Global / the French consulate up to 6 months before travel. Allows stays of up to 90 days within any 180-day period across the Schengen area. Standard visa fee is EUR 90 (lower for children); travel insurance with EUR 30,000 medical coverage and proof of accommodation and funds are required.

Indian nationals can apply for a Japan eVisa for short-term tourism through the official JAPAN eVISA portal (mofa.go.jp) when applying via a registered travel agency. The single-entry tourist eVisa permits stays of up to 90 days and the consular visa fee is currently waived; a printed visa issuance notice must be presented at immigration.

Indian passport holders require an e-Visa to enter Singapore. Apply online through an authorised visa agent or Strategic Visa Issuance System (SAVE) before travel; multiple-entry visas are typically valid up to 2 years with a maximum stay of 30 days per visit.
